_publications._publicationTitle: Global proteomics and affinity mass spectrometry analysis of human Schwann cells indicates that variation in and loss of neurofibromin (NF1) alters protein expression and cellular and mitochondrial metabolism.

_contextSnippet: NADH-linked OXPHOS and electron transfer capacity was ~ 1.5fold lower in NF1 -/- cells compared to NF1 +/+ (Fig. 5 C-E)
details: NF1 knockout cells showed 1.5-fold lower NADH-linked OXPHOS and electron transfer capacity compared to wild-type cells
